req.title: System-Admin views department
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-008 requires an authenticated system-admin to view a department via the UI at /departments. Key coverage axes are: (1) happy-path for a valid admin viewing an existing department with audit record written (EP), (2) unauthenticated access attempt returning 401 (NEG), (3) viewing a department whose name sits at the maximum allowed character boundary (BVA), (4) a role-forbidden user (non-admin) receiving 403 (DT decision on role), and (5) state transition from unauthenticated -> authenticated -> department-viewed confirming audit trail (STC). Five tests are generated to satisfy the minimum-three requirement with richer coverage.

tests.name: test_admin_views_existing_department_returns_200_and_audit_record
preconditions: At least one department record exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /departments and select an existing department
Capture the HTTP response status and returned entity id
Query the audit log for a matching VIEW event
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the view action by system-admin
trace: R-WEB-008: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_views_existing_department_returns_200_and_audit_record(
    admin_session,
    existing_department,
    audit_log_client,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    response = admin_session.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert response.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("id") == dept_id, "Entity id mismatch in response"
    assert body.get("name"), "Department name must be present"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch(
        entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written for VIEW"
    latest = audit_entries[-1]
    assert latest["actor_role"] == "system-admin"
    assert latest["entity_type"] == "department"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_department_returns_401
preconditions: No active session / authentication token is present
At least one department record exists in the system
actions: Send GET request to /departments/{id} without credentials
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_department_returns_401(
    anon_client,
    existing_department,
    audit_log_client,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    before_count = len(
        audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )

    response = anon_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.text
    assert "id" not in body or dept_id not in body, (
        "Department data must not be exposed to unauthenticated caller"
    )

    after_count = len(
        audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )
    assert after_count == before_count, (
        "Audit record must NOT be written on unauthenticated access"
    )

tests.name: test_view_department_with_max_length_name_returns_200
preconditions: A department exists whose name length equals the system maximum (255 chars)
actions: Ensure a department with a 255-character name is seeded via <seed_department helper>
Capture response status and verify name length in payload
expected: Response body name field is exactly 255 characters
Audit record written for VIEW action
trace: R-WEB-008: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NAME_LEN = 255

def test_view_department_with_max_length_name_returns_200(
    admin_session,
    seed_department,
    audit_log_client,
):
    max_name = "A" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    dept = seed_department(name=max_name)
    dept_id = dept["id"]

    response = admin_session.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert response.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert len(body["name"]) == MAX_NAME_LEN, (
        f"Name length should be {MAX_NAME_LEN}, got {len(body['name'])}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.fetch(
        entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW"
    )
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_cannot_view_department_returns_403
preconditions: At least one department record exists
actions: Login as employee-role user via <employee_session fixture>
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: No audit record is written for this access attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_role_cannot_view_department_returns_403(
    employee_session,
    existing_department,
    audit_log_client,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]
    before_count = len(
        audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )

    response = employee_session.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("error") or body.get("message"), (
        "403 response must include an error/message field"
    )

    after_count = len(
        audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )
    assert after_count == before_count, (
        "Audit record must NOT be written on forbidden access"
    )

tests.name: test_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_authenticated_enables_view_and_audit
preconditions: Session starts with no credentials (unauthenticated state)
At least one department record exists
actions: Attempt to view /departments/{id} without auth -> expect 401 (state: unauthenticated)
Attempt to view /departments/{id} with valid session -> expect 200 (state: authenticated-and-viewed)
Verify audit record written after successful view
expected: Audit record is present only after authenticated view, not before
trace: R-WEB-008: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_authenticated_enables_view_and_audit(
    anon_client,
    login,
    existing_department,
    audit_log_client,
):
    dept_id = existing_department["id"]

    # State: unauthenticated
    unauth_resp = anon_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert unauth_resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Unauthenticated state: expected 401, got {unauth_resp.status_code}"
    )
    before_audit = len(
        audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )

    # Transition: authenticate
    authed_session = login(role="system-admin")

    # State: authenticated-and-viewed
    auth_resp = authed_session.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Authenticated state: expected 200, got {auth_resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert auth_resp.json()["id"] == dept_id

    after_audit = len(
        audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=dept_id, action="VIEW")
    )
    assert after_audit == before_audit + 1, (
        "Exactly one new audit record expected after authenticated view"
    )
